div.head: The Ambassador in Argentina (Armour) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Buenos Aires, April 8, 1943—6 p.m.
[Received April 9—1:24 a.m.]

div.p:
752. My telegram No. 711, April 2, 6 p.m.Not printed. Exclusion of Argentina from invitation to post-war food and monetary conferencesFor correspondence regarding postwar financial arrangements, see pp. 1054 ff.
has caused a profound impression here, although owing state of siege regulations press comment has, with few exceptions which are being reported by airmail, been extremely guarded.

One of my colleagues informs me that the Finance MinisterCarlos Alberto Acevedo. professed pained surprise that Argentina, one of the world’s greatest grain and meat producing countries, should not have been invited to the food conference.
